channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Steven Horsford to hand off role as chair of Congressional Black Caucus
item.description: Nevada Congressman Steven Horsford will no longer be the chair of the Congressional Black Caucus for the 119th Congress.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California lawmakers kick off special session to 'Trump-proof' the Golden State
item.description: California lawmakers on Monday are kicking off a special legislative session in what Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) has deemed efforts to "Trump-proof" the Golden State from potential federal interference.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Fani Willis ordered to turn over communications in Trump case to conservative group
item.description: A Georgia judge ordered Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis to turn over to a conservative watchdog group any communications she may have with special counsel Jack Smith or the now-disbanded House Jan. 6 committee.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Unlikely battleground California plays key role -- again -- in setting US House's political balance
item.description: Democrats and Republicans agreed on at least one thing before November's election: California would play a central role in determining which party controls the U.S. House.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Blood, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Democrat Jerry Nadler steps aside from top Judiciary role, avoiding party fight
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Jerry Nadler announced Wednesday that he will be stepping down as the top Democrat on the House Judiciary Committee next year, avoiding an intra-party fight over a prominent position as the party prepares to confront a second Trump presidency.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Gunning for the 2nd Amendment
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump seeks to have Georgia election case dismissed, citing presidential immunity
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is trying to get the Georgia election interference case against him dismissed, asserting that the state's courts will not have jurisdiction over him once he returns to the White House next month.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sotomayor compares trans medical 'treatments' to aspirin in question about side effects during oral arguments
item.description: Justice Sonia Sotomayor said "every medical treatment has a risk, even taking aspirin" during oral arguments in the high-profile SCOTUS case involving trans "treatments" for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court appears divided over state bans on gender transition 'treatments' for minors
item.description: The Supreme Court appeared divided Wednesday over the constitutionality of state laws banning gender transition medical treatments for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominates Paul Atkins as SEC chair
item.description: “He also recognizes that digital assets & other innovations are crucial to Making America Greater than Ever Before,” the president-elect added.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, NewsNation

item.title: Migrants nervous over Trump plans for mass deportations
item.description: "I am traumatized. If I don't get the appointment (with border officials) I will go back," one woman said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: AP Decision Notes: What to expect in Louisiana's general election
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Louisiana voters on Saturday will decide the fates of four proposed amendments to the state constitution, in the final statewide election of 2024.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT YOON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Inside RFK Jr.'s nonprofit's legal battles over vaccines and public health
item.description: The nonprofit Children's Health Defense that Kennedy led has filed nearly 30 federal and state lawsuits since 2020, many challenging vaccines and public health mandates.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Garland, DOJ leads ask staffers to stay through Trump term: Report
item.description: Attorney General Merrick Garland is one of the top Justice Department officials asking staffers to stay with the agency, reports say.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Condo deadline looms: Owners must confront years of neglect
item.description: The condo conundrum continues as the December 31st inspection deadline is only a few weeks away. State lawmakers promise action on the issue next session, but the clock is ticking.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump and Republican senators plan agenda for first 30 days
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump dialed in to what was described as a “love fest” Tuesday with Senate Republicans as they begin laying the groundwork for control of government in the new year, and a potential first-30-days agenda.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers urge judge to toss his hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers formally asked a judge Monday to throw out his hush money criminal conviction, arguing that continuing the case would present unconstitutional “disruptions to the institution of the Presidency.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's more than $50 billion pay deal at Tesla was rejected again. Here is why
item.description: A judge in Delaware has for the second time struck down a compensation package for Elon Musk after a Tesla shareholder filed suit.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Senate Democrats stick with Schumer as leader
item.description: Chuck Schumer faced no opposition in the party leadership elections, in which Illinois Sen. Dick Durbin was also re-elected to the No. 2 spot and Minnesota Sen. Amy Klobuchar became the new No. 3.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Judge closes Hunter Biden gun case after presidential pardon
item.description: Hunter Biden’s federal gun case was brought to a close by a judge Tuesday following the presidential pardon by his father. In a terse order, U.S. District Judge Maryellen Noreika terminated all proceedings in the case, citing President Biden’s executive grant of clemency to his son.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota House panel votes to absorb legal fees of DFLer in employment dispute
item.description: A DFL state lawmaker will have legal fees covered by the Minnesota House after alleging her rights were violated by an outside employer. Republicans say the move sets a bad precedent.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst, MPR News

item.title: The fight against medical debt is pivoting to the states after Trump's election
item.description: Patient and consumer advocates fear a new Trump administration will scale back federal efforts to expand financial protections for patients and shield them from debt.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: Wave of Democrats slam Biden's pardon of his son Hunter
item.description: While some Democrats extended a measure of sympathy, President Biden has taken heat on several fronts.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominates Kash Patel to lead FBI
item.description: President-elect Trump plans to nominate Kash Patel to serve as head of the FBI, elevating a staunch ally and controversial figure who sparked internal battles during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau flew to Florida on Friday to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs threaten to crack open North American economies
item.description: MEXICO CITY — President-elect Trump’s pledge to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada threatens to upend decades of North American integration, a bumpy process that’s recast the two U.S. neighbors as providers of raw and finished goods for the world’s biggest economy.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic lawmakers report Thanksgiving bomb threats against their homes
item.description: At least five Democratic members of Congress from Connecticut were targeted by bomb threats on Thursday. Police who responded said they found no evidence of explosives on the lawmakers' properties.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News
